name: Château de Montréal - Peyrehorade Loop
name_original: Château de Montréal – boucle à Peyrehorade
description: Hike a moderate 9.2-mile (14.8 km) loop around Peyrehorade, exploring the historic Château de Montréal and varied landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Peyrehorade offers access to a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical sites. The region features a mix of agricultural land, woodlands, and paths connecting ancient abbeys and bastide towns. Elevation changes are generally gentle, with most routes featuring modest climbs. The terrain provides varied walking experiences suitable for different activity levels. Best hiking trails around Peyrehorade The most popular hiking route is Château de Montréal - Peyrehorade Loop, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route explores the local countryside and historical points of interest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Abbaye de Sordes – Heritage loop from Sorde-l'Abbaye, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path. This trail features heritage sites and views of the surrounding rural landscape. Local hikers also love the Porte de Hastingues – Bastide of Hastingues loop from Hastingues, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through historical bastide towns and local fields, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Hiking around Peyrehorade is defined by historical sites, river valleys, and gentle rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Peyrehorade are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Peyrehorade's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Peyrehorade?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Peyrehorade, offering a variety of experiences. These include 11 easy routes, 18 moderate trails, and 4 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Peyrehorade?
A: The landscape around Peyrehorade is characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical sites. You'll find a mix of agricultural land, woodlands, and paths connecting ancient abbeys and bastide towns. Elevation changes are generally gentle, making for accessible and pleasant walks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Peyrehorade?
A: Yes, Peyrehorade offers several easy routes suitable for families. A good option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36034101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porte de Hastingues – Bastide of Hastingues loop from Hastingues&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.1 miles (6.6 km) long and leads through historical bastide towns and local fields. Another easy choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10524446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bastide of Hastingues loop from Abbaye d'Arthous&lt;/a&gt;, covering 4.0 miles (6.4 km).

Q: Can I find circular walks around Peyrehorade?
A: Many of the trails around Peyrehorade are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24661851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de Sordes – Heritage loop from Sorde-l'Abbaye&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path that explores heritage sites and rural landscapes in a loop.

Q: What interesting historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bastide of Hastingues&lt;/a&gt; or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6036001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hastingues Village&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24661855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorbe l'abbaye abbey – Bourg-Neuf Washhouse Fountain loop from Sorde-l'Abbaye&lt;/a&gt; will take you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6584706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bourg-Neuf Washhouse Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into local heritage.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While many routes feature gentle elevation, there are 4 trails classified as difficult for those seeking more of a challenge. These routes typically involve longer distances or more significant climbs, providing a more strenuous experience within the rolling landscape.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Peyrehorade?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the region for its historical sites, the peaceful river valleys, and the gentle rolling hills that define the landscape, offering varied terrain for all abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options around Peyrehorade?
A: For those looking for longer excursions, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996596068/chateau-de-montreal-peyrehorade-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Montréal - Peyrehorade Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a notable option. This moderate trail spans 9.2 miles (14.8 km) and typically takes around 4 hours to complete, exploring the local countryside and historical points of interest.

Q: Is Peyrehorade a good destination for winter hiking?
A: The region's generally gentle elevation changes and mix of woodlands and agricultural land can make it suitable for winter hiking, provided conditions are not icy or excessively wet. Always check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out during colder months.

Q: Are there any trails that follow river paths or offer views of water?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by river valleys. You can find paths that follow waterways, such as sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adour Towpath&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views alongside the river. Many routes incorporate views of the surrounding rural landscape and its natural water features.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Peyrehorade?
A: Peyrehorade's mild climate generally makes it pleasant for hiking from spring through autumn. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while autumn offers comfortable temperatures and vibrant colors. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are often preferred.
tips: The Château de Montréal has had many functions since its construction in the 1550s. Originally built to control river traffic on the Gaves united, it later became the residence of the Viscounts of the Orthe region, before becoming a military hospital, a convent and a technical college. It is however today the town hall of Peyrehorade.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Château de Montréal – loop at Peyrehorade?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as a moderate hike. It requires good fitness, and sure-footedness is recommended due to varied surfaces along the route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop is approximately 14.8 kilometers (9.2 miles) long and generally takes around 4 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the loop?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied surfaces. You'll encounter significant portions of paved sections, unpaved areas, and asphalt. There are also segments on streets, roads, and dedicated paths, with less than 100 meters specifically designated as a 'hiking path'.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historical Montréal Castle or Orthe Castle. This 16th-century castle, now housing the town hall of Peyrehorade, is a significant historical monument with robust round towers and defensive ramparts. You can explore its exterior and appreciate its rich history.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking lots aren't detailed, the trail starts and loops around Peyrehorade, where the Château de Montréal is located. You can typically find parking within the commune of Peyrehorade, near the castle or town center.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Information regarding direct public transport options to the trailhead is not readily available. It's advisable to check local transport services for Peyrehorade if you plan to rely on public transport.

faqs.question: Is the Château de Montréal – loop at Peyrehorade dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain which includes streets and roads, it's recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas or near traffic.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Château de Montréal – loop at Peyrehorade. The trail is freely accessible.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The Landes department generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, making for a more comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sentier Aspremont 2, Sentier Aspremont 1, Promenade des Tilhoulès, and Chemin de Gardéra.

faqs.question: Are there any specific historical features of the Château de Montréal worth noting?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Beyond its impressive exterior, the castle has a rich history, having served as a military hospital, convent, and technical college. It features 16th-century sculptures within its chapel and is rumored to have underground chambers and vaulted passages carved by water, which may have provided water access during sieges.
